reasoning: Both the LLM fidelity analysis and the programmatic detectors identified a major structural issue where a single table spanning two pages was split into two separate table objects instead of being merged into a continuous table. Since there is exactly one major issue, count-based rules dictate routing to HUMAN_REVIEW to fix the table structure.

concerns.analysis: The source document contains a single large table that spans across both pages (pages 1 and 2) with repeated headers. The extraction rules explicitly state that when a large table spans multiple PDF pages with repeated headers, it should be rendered as one continuous table. However, the output JSON splits the table into two separate `table` objects (one on page 1 and one on page 2).
concerns.source_pdf_quote: A single continuous table spanning pages 1 and 2 with repeated headers starting with 'Case Number | Owner - Facility | ...'
concerns.output_quote: Two separate objects with 'type': 'table', one inside pages[0].content and another inside pages[1].content.

concerns.description: Table split across page break(s): 1 table(s) with repeated headers across consecutive pages
concerns.analysis: split_table_count=1. Examples: pages 1-2: 12 cols (case number, owner - facility, permit number...). Tables spanning multiple pages were extracted as separate table elements with duplicated header rows. The HTML output will show multiple tables instead of one continuous table — a structural accessibility issue.

concerns.description: Repeated footer/header text (2x): "dwq january 2012 civil penalty assessments"
concerns.analysis: Short text with footer-like pattern appears on 2 pages

action_items: Merge the separate table objects on pages 1 and 2 into a single continuous table.
Remove the duplicated header row from the second part of the table.
